title: Witheringia coccoloboides
taxonomy: Witheringia coccoloboides is a species of flowering plant in the family Solanaceae, commonly known as the nightshade family. The plant is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Its classification is further divided into the genus Witheringia, which is a group of flowering plants that are closely related to other species within the Solanaceae family. The exact classification of Witheringia coccoloboides is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Solanales, family Solanaceae, genus Witheringia, species W. coccoloboides. This classification helps to understand the plant's evolutionary relationships with other species within the plant kingdom.

intro: Witheringia coccoloboides, commonly known as "Ecuadorian guanabani," is a plant species native to the tropical regions of South America, belonging to the family Solanaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Witheringia coccoloboides include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health issues.
